signals.title: NZD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Geopolitical De-escalation
signals.text: NZD outperformance (1W avg +1.64%) combined with JPY weakness (-0.66%) creates a 2.30% G8 spread that is likely to widen as US-Iran ceasefire progress fuels risk-on sentiment. The 150bp NZD/JPY nominal carry advantage and JPY COT net short -81.6k reinforce the upside bias as safe-haven demand for JPY evaporates. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 45% hike priced) on 16 June is the primary catalyst for any structural reversal of this trend.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Crowded Long Unwind Risk vs Carry Support
signals.text: AUD COT net long +57.1k (74.5% long) creates a crowded-long unwind risk despite the 73bp OIS carry advantage favoring the AUD. While US-Iran deal optimism supports risk-sensitive currencies, the extreme retail short positioning (80% short) suggests a contrarian squeeze is likely to target the 0.7200 level. The next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 16 June determines if the current hiking bias can sustain these levels against the heavy positioning.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Yield Gap vs Squeeze Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY closed Friday at 159.25, supported by a 289bp OIS carry advantage and a bull steepener regime in the US curve (+46.3bp 2s10s). However, the JPY COT net short of -81.6k and a positive JPY CESI (+12) suggest the pair is vulnerable to a sharp downside squeeze if BoJ hike bets (79% probability) continue to firm. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 45% hike priced) on 16 June is the catalyst for resolving the tension between the yield gap and spec positioning.

signals.title: USD/CAD — COT Short Overhang + Growth Divergence
signals.text: CAD COT net short -41.7k reinforces downside pressure on the Loonie following weak Canadian GDP data and a softening USD. The 138bp OIS carry advantage for USD keeps the floor under the pair, but the restrictive US real rate (+1.22%) vs CAD (+0.18%) suggests a widening policy divergence. The next BoC meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 10 June is the catalyst for confirming if the growth slowdown warrants a more accommodative stance.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Retail Extreme vs Real Rate Parity
signals.text: USD/CHF faces contrarian reversal risk as retail positioning reaches an 85% long extreme while the pair closed Friday at 0.7808. Despite a massive 375bp nominal carry advantage for the USD, the real rate gap is significantly narrower (USD +1.22% vs CHF -0.31%), limiting the fundamental drive for further upside. The next SNB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 18 June determines if the neutral market bias shifts toward supporting the franc.
